title: Plan a Stage 7 Mathematics Exhibition
overview: Bring together everything from Stage 7 Mathematics to design a mathematics exhibition that teaches visitors the most important ideas from this stage.

project.drivingQuestion: How can we use the mathematics from Stage 7 to plan a clear, accurate and welcoming exhibition for our school or community?
project.finalProduct: Create an exhibition plan with labelled displays, a schedule, a budget, a visitor survey with a data display, and a short explanation of your mathematical decisions.

stages.title: 1. Term 1 foundations display
stages.prompt: Choose the two most important ideas from Term 1 (Units 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6). Build a display that teaches each idea with a model, an example and a check question.
stages.evidence: Two labelled displays with models, examples and check questions

stages.title: 2. Term 2 applications corner
stages.prompt: Design an activity corner where visitors use Term 2 skills (Units 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12) to solve one real problem, showing every step of the method.
stages.evidence: A worked real problem with each method step shown

stages.title: 3. Term 3 connections wall
stages.prompt: Create a connections wall that links Term 3 ideas (Units 13, 14, 15, 16) to earlier units, with at least three labelled connections.
stages.evidence: A connections wall with three labelled links between units

stages.title: 4. Present and explain
stages.prompt: Present your exhibition plan. Explain at least three mathematical choices, check that your answers are sensible and identify one improvement you would make.
stages.evidence: Spoken, written or recorded mathematical explanation

evidenceChecklist: A worked real problem with method steps
A connections wall with three links
A schedule and budget for the exhibition
A survey with organised data display

rubric.secure: Calculations, measures, shapes, data and models are accurate and checked.
The plan connects ideas from several Stage 7 units for a clear purpose.
Labels, diagrams, tables or charts make the mathematics visible.
Decisions are explained using appropriate mathematical words and evidence.

questions.question: What does “Integer” mean?
options: A positive whole number, a negative whole number, or zero
The number the same distance from zero on the other side
Distance from zero; always positive or zero
The result of multiplying a number by an integer
questions.answer: A positive whole number, a negative whole number, or zero
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Integer means A positive whole number, a negative whole number, or zero.

questions.question: What does “Opposite” mean?
options: The number the same distance from zero on the other side
Distance from zero; always positive or zero
The result of multiplying a number by an integer
A number that divides exactly into another
questions.answer: The number the same distance from zero on the other side
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Opposite means The number the same distance from zero on the other side.

questions.question: What does “Variable” mean?
options: A letter standing for an unknown number
The number multiplying a variable
A number or product of numbers and letters
A term that is just a number
questions.answer: A letter standing for an unknown number
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Variable means A letter standing for an unknown number.

questions.question: What does “Coefficient” mean?
options: The number multiplying a variable
A number or product of numbers and letters
A term that is just a number
Terms joined by + or −, with no equals sign
questions.answer: The number multiplying a variable
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Coefficient means The number multiplying a variable.

questions.question: What does “Power of 10” mean?
options: 10 raised to a whole-number power, e.g. 10³ = 1000
The raised number showing the power
A digit position after the decimal point
A meaningful digit, counted from the first non-zero digit
questions.answer: 10 raised to a whole-number power, e.g. 10³ = 1000
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Power of 10 means 10 raised to a whole-number power, e.g. 10³ = 1000.

questions.question: What does “Exponent” mean?
options: The raised number showing the power
A digit position after the decimal point
A meaningful digit, counted from the first non-zero digit
Replacing a number with a nearby, simpler one
questions.answer: The raised number showing the power
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Exponent means The raised number showing the power.

questions.question: What does “Decimal point” mean?
options: The dot separating wholes from parts
The first, second and third places after the point
A zero at the end that does not change the value
Result of multiplying / of dividing
questions.answer: The dot separating wholes from parts
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Decimal point means The dot separating wholes from parts.

questions.question: What does “Tenths / Hundredths / Thousandths” mean?
options: The first, second and third places after the point
A zero at the end that does not change the value
Result of multiplying / of dividing
Number being divided / number you divide by
questions.answer: The first, second and third places after the point
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Tenths / Hundredths / Thousandths means The first, second and third places after the point.

questions.question: What does “Vertex” mean?
options: The point where two lines or arms meet
Add to 180° / add to 90°
Equal angles opposite where two lines cross
questions.answer: The point where two lines or arms meet
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Vertex means The point where two lines or arms meet.

questions.question: What does “Acute / Obtuse / Reflex” mean?
options: Add to 180° / add to 90°
Equal angles opposite where two lines cross
A line crossing two or more other lines
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Acute / Obtuse / Reflex means Under 90° / 90°–180° / 180°–360°.

questions.question: What does “Population” mean?
options: The whole group being studied
A smaller part of the population
Unfairness that makes results lean the wrong way
Data in groups or names
questions.answer: The whole group being studied
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Population means The whole group being studied.

questions.question: What does “Sample” mean?
options: A smaller part of the population
Unfairness that makes results lean the wrong way
Data in groups or names
Numerical data you count (whole, separate)
questions.answer: A smaller part of the population
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sample means A smaller part of the population.

questions.question: What does “Numerator” mean?
options: The top number – how many parts you have
The bottom number – how many equal parts in the whole
Value less than 1 / value 1 or more (top-heavy)
A whole number and a proper fraction together
questions.answer: The top number – how many parts you have
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Numerator means The top number – how many parts you have.

questions.question: What does “Denominator” mean?
options: The bottom number – how many equal parts in the whole
Value less than 1 / value 1 or more (top-heavy)
A whole number and a proper fraction together
Different fractions with the same value
questions.answer: The bottom number – how many equal parts in the whole
questions.hint: Use the Unit 7 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Denominator means The bottom number – how many equal parts in the whole.

questions.question: What does “Polygon” mean?
options: A closed 2D shape made of straight sides
All sides and angles equal / not all equal
Centre to edge / right across through the centre
The distance around a circle
questions.answer: A closed 2D shape made of straight sides
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Polygon means A closed 2D shape made of straight sides.

questions.question: What does “Regular / Irregular” mean?
options: All sides and angles equal / not all equal
Centre to edge / right across through the centre
The distance around a circle
Angle inside a corner / on a straight line outside it
questions.answer: All sides and angles equal / not all equal
questions.hint: Use the Unit 8 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Regular / Irregular means All sides and angles equal / not all equal.

questions.question: What does “Sequence” mean?
options: An ordered list of numbers following a rule
A number in the sequence / where it sits (n)
How to get from one term to the next
How to find a term from its position
questions.answer: An ordered list of numbers following a rule
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Sequence means An ordered list of numbers following a rule.

questions.question: What does “Term / Position” mean?
options: A number in the sequence / where it sits (n)
How to get from one term to the next
How to find a term from its position
The constant step in an arithmetic sequence
questions.answer: A number in the sequence / where it sits (n)
questions.hint: Use the Unit 9 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Term / Position means A number in the sequence / where it sits (n).

questions.question: What does “Percentage” mean?
options: A number of parts out of 100
The decimal you multiply by to apply a change
Growth or fall as a percentage of the original
(new minus original) / original × 100%
questions.answer: A number of parts out of 100
questions.hint: Use the Unit 10 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Percentage means A number of parts out of 100.

questions.question: What does “Multiplier” mean?
options: The decimal you multiply by to apply a change
Growth or fall as a percentage of the original
(new minus original) / original × 100%
Finding the original amount before a change
questions.answer: The decimal you multiply by to apply a change
questions.hint: Use the Unit 10 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Multiplier means The decimal you multiply by to apply a change.

questions.question: What does “Coordinates” mean?
options: An ordered pair (x, y) giving a position
The point (0, 0) where the axes cross
One of the four regions of the plane
Steepness: change in y over change in x
questions.answer: An ordered pair (x, y) giving a position
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Coordinates means An ordered pair (x, y) giving a position.

questions.question: What does “Origin” mean?
options: The point (0, 0) where the axes cross
One of the four regions of the plane
Steepness: change in y over change in x
Where a line crosses the y-axis (x = 0)
questions.answer: The point (0, 0) where the axes cross
questions.hint: Use the Unit 11 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Origin means The point (0, 0) where the axes cross.

questions.question: What does “Ratio” mean?
options: A comparison of quantities in the same unit
One of the numbers in a ratio
Divide all terms by the HCF
Different ratios showing the same relationship
questions.answer: A comparison of quantities in the same unit
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Ratio means A comparison of quantities in the same unit.

questions.question: What does “Term” mean?
options: One of the numbers in a ratio
Divide all terms by the HCF
Different ratios showing the same relationship
A statement that two ratios are equal
questions.answer: One of the numbers in a ratio
questions.hint: Use the Unit 12 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Term means One of the numbers in a ratio.

questions.question: What does “Outcome” mean?
options: One possible result of an experiment
One or more outcomes we are interested in
The set of all possible outcomes
Probability worked out by reasoning
questions.answer: One possible result of an experiment
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Outcome means One possible result of an experiment.

questions.question: What does “Event” mean?
options: One or more outcomes we are interested in
The set of all possible outcomes
Probability worked out by reasoning
Probability found from doing trials
questions.answer: One or more outcomes we are interested in
questions.hint: Use the Unit 13 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Event means One or more outcomes we are interested in.

questions.question: What does “Coordinates” mean?
options: An ordered pair (x, y) giving position
The centre point (0, 0) where the axes cross
One of the four regions of the grid
A slide described by a column vector
questions.answer: An ordered pair (x, y) giving position
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Coordinates means An ordered pair (x, y) giving position.

questions.question: What does “Origin” mean?
options: The centre point (0, 0) where the axes cross
One of the four regions of the grid
A slide described by a column vector
A flip across a mirror line
questions.answer: The centre point (0, 0) where the axes cross
questions.hint: Use the Unit 14 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Origin means The centre point (0, 0) where the axes cross.

questions.question: What does “Area” mean?
options: The flat space a 2D shape covers (square units)
The distance around the edge of a shape
The space a 3D solid fills (cubic units)
The total area of all the faces of a solid
questions.answer: The flat space a 2D shape covers (square units)
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Area means The flat space a 2D shape covers (square units).

questions.question: What does “Perimeter” mean?
options: The distance around the edge of a shape
The space a 3D solid fills (cubic units)
The total area of all the faces of a solid
The height measured at a right angle to the base
questions.answer: The distance around the edge of a shape
questions.hint: Use the Unit 15 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Perimeter means The distance around the edge of a shape.

questions.question: What does “Mean” mean?
options: Total of values divided by how many there are
The middle value of ordered data
The most frequently occurring value
Highest value minus lowest value
questions.answer: Total of values divided by how many there are
questions.hint: Use the Unit 16 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Mean means Total of values divided by how many there are.

questions.question: What does “Median” mean?
options: The middle value of ordered data
The most frequently occurring value
Highest value minus lowest value
Values splitting ordered data into four equal parts
questions.answer: The middle value of ordered data
questions.hint: Use the Unit 16 Math Words & Symbols reference.
questions.explanation: Median means The middle value of ordered data.
